title: Endometrial Hyperplasia with Atypia
chief_complaint: I'm going through menopause but I've been having heavy irregular bleeding for the past 4 months. I soak through a pad in an hour. I haven't had a period in 2 years before this. I'm obese, have diabetes, and never had children.

physical_exam.Pelvic: Uterus normal size, mobile. No adnexal masses. No cervical lesions.
physical_exam.Labs: Hb 8.5 (iron deficiency anemia from chronic blood loss), MCV 72

hidden_data.tvus: Endometrial thickness 18mm (postmenopausal >5mm is abnormal). Heterogeneous endometrium. No focal mass. No myometrial invasion.
hidden_data.endometrial_biopsy: Endometrial hyperplasia with atypia (EIN - Endometrioid Intraepithelial Neoplasia). No invasive carcinoma in this specimen.
hidden_data.hysteroscopy: Thickened, irregular endometrium. Directed biopsies taken.
hidden_data.genetic: No Lynch syndrome testing yet (should be offered if <50 with endometrial cancer/precursor)

correct_diagnosis: Endometrial Hyperplasia with Atypia (EIN) in Postmenopausal Woman with Risk Factors (Obesity, Diabetes, Nulliparity)
key_learning_points: Endometrial hyperplasia = proliferation of endometrial glands. Classified as: Without atypia (low cancer risk <5% over 20 years), With atypia/EIN (high cancer risk 30-40% progresses to or coexists with endometrial cancer)

Risk factors for endometrial hyperplasia/cancer: UNOPPOSED ESTROGEN (most important): Obesity (peripheral conversion of androgens to estrogen), PCOS, Nulliparity, Early menarche/Late menopause, Estrogen-only HRT, Tamoxifen. Lynch syndrome (hereditary - 60% lifetime risk of endometrial cancer)

Postmenopausal bleeding: ANY vaginal bleeding after menopause is endometrial cancer until proven otherwise. Workup: Transvaginal ultrasound (endometrial stripe ≤4-5mm on adequate exam = low risk. If >5mm or inadequate visualization → endometrial biopsy). Endometrial biopsy (Pipelle) in office - 90-95% sensitivity for endometrial cancer. Hysteroscopy + D&C if biopsy non-diagnostic or persistent symptoms

Management: WITHOUT ATYPIA: Progestin therapy (Levonorgestrel IUS - Mirena first-line, Medroxyprogesterone acetate 10-20mg daily ×14 days/month). Re-biopsy in 3-6 months. Regression rate >90% with progestins. WITH ATYPIA (EIN): Total hysterectomy + BSO (standard - 30-40% risk of concurrent endometrial carcinoma). If desires fertility (rare in this age group): High-dose progestin therapy (Megace 80-160mg BID or Mirena IUS) with close surveillance (biopsy q3-6 months). Must complete childbearing then hysterectomy

Prevention: Weight loss (reduces peripheral estrogen), Treating underlying conditions (PCOS - OCPs or cyclic progestins to induce regular shedding), Mirena IUS in high-risk women. Never give unopposed estrogen to women with a uterus (must add progestin for endometrial protection)
